title: High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026: Youth Applications Open to Attend UN Summit in New York (Fully Global Policy Engagement Opportunity)
summary: Youth applications are now open for the 2026 High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) , taking place from July 7–15, 2026 at United Nations Headquarters in New York City . This is a prestigious opportunity for young people in Canada to engage directly with global leaders, policymakers, and development practitioners working to advance the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) . What is the High-Level Political Forum (HLPF)? The High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) is the United Nations’ central platform for: Reviewing global progress on the 2030 Agenda Monitoring implementation of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Promoting accountability and policy coordination across countries Driving forward inclusive, sustainable, and evidence-based solutions Each year, HLPF convenes: Heads of State and Government Ministers and UN officials Civil society organizations Youth leaders and innovators Private sector representatives Why You Should Apply for HLPF 2026 HLPF 2026 represents a critical moment to accelerate global action toward the SDGs as the 2030 deadline approaches. Selected youth participants will have the opportunity to: Participate in high-level policy discussions at the United Nations Engage with global leaders and decision-makers Contribute to international dialogue on sustainable development Showcase community-driven solutions and local innovations Strengthen networks with global youth advocates and organizations Gain exposure to global governance and multilateral processes Eligibility Criteria To apply for HLPF 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or individual with refugee status Reside in Canada for the duration of the programme and event Be aged 18–30 years at the time of the event Demonstrate active engagement in advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) through: Community initiatives Academic research Advocacy work Entrepreneurship Innovative projects Important Notes Individuals temporarily in Canada (e.g., students or tourists on non-immigrant visas) are not eligible Applicants living outside Canada are not eligible If a U.S. visa is required, applicants must indicate this in their application While support letters may be provided, applicants are responsible for meeting all U.S. entry requirements What Makes a Strong Application? Strong candidates typically demonstrate: Clear commitment to sustainable development and SDG impact Evidence of leadership and initiative Experience in community or global engagement A focus on equity, inclusion, and collaboration Ability to translate local impact into global relevance Application Deadline Deadline: April 6, 2026 (11:45 PM) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted approximately two weeks after the deadline How to Apply Submit your application here: https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=Rs4xYpvFukaie_YKxYkOFyr00ELyvRhBmoHhpGlQ_59UQjc2WFhFQkJRWkdLRUFaR0NXMVJBNTVKVi4u origin=lprLink route=shorturl The High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026 is one of the most influential global platforms for shaping sustainable development policy. For young leaders in Canada, this is a rare opportunity to move from local action to global influence—bringing grassroots solutions to the world stage at the United Nations. If you are passionate about driving impact, influencing policy, and contributing to the SDGs, this is an opportunity you should not miss. title: AI Policy Lead at J-PAL (MIT): Shape the Future of AI for Social Good
summary: Are you passionate about using artificial intelligence to drive meaningful social impact? The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) is hiring an AI Policy Lead to support its expanding work on AI for social good. This exciting opportunity is based at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in Cambridge , Massachusetts , United States , and offers a hybrid work structure. About the Role The AI Policy Lead will play a key role at the intersection of technology, research, and policymaking. This position focuses on ensuring that AI tools are developed, evaluated, and implemented in ways that are evidence-based and socially impactful. Working within J-PAL’s Policy Communications team, the successful candidate will help shape how AI is used to address global development challenges. Key Responsibilities The AI Policy Lead will be responsible for driving initiatives that connect cutting-edge research with real-world policy applications. Core responsibilities include: Leading efforts to evaluate and scale promising AI tools Building partnerships with governments, researchers, and technology companies Ensuring AI adoption decisions are grounded in rigorous, credible evidence Supporting policy engagement and communication strategies Contributing to research-driven policymaking efforts This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across sectors and translating research into practical solutions. Why This Role Matters Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries and societies. Ensuring that AI is used responsibly and effectively—especially in development contexts—is critical. This role provides a unique opportunity to influence how AI is applied to solve real-world problems, particularly in areas such as poverty reduction, public policy, and global development. Benefits of the Role Working at J-PAL offers a chance to collaborate with leading researchers and policymakers around the world. Benefits include: Exposure to cutting-edge AI and policy work Opportunity to influence global development outcomes Collaboration with top researchers and institutions Hybrid work environment Professional growth in a globally recognized research organization Who Should Apply This role is ideal for professionals with a strong interest in AI, public policy, and social impact. Candidates who are passionate about bridging technology and policy, and who thrive in collaborative, research-driven environments, are encouraged to apply. Application Details Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. Apply here For more Policy Opportunities from OFY, Click here. title: India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026: Transform Your Impact in Climate Leadership
summary: Applications are now open for the India Leadership Academy for Climate Leaders (ILACL) 2026 , a high-impact leadership programme designed to equip emerging climate leaders with the skills needed to navigate complex systems and drive meaningful change. This initiative is led by Common Purpose India in partnership with YES Foundation , with venue support from Samagata Foundation . As climate challenges become increasingly central to economic growth, public health, and social equity, this programme focuses on strengthening leadership capacity rather than technical knowledge—empowering participants to lead effectively in high-pressure, resource-constrained environments. About the Programme The ILACL is a 4-day, in-person leadership experience designed for individuals working across climate-related sectors. Held in Bangalore , India , the programme brings together a diverse cohort of leaders tackling challenges in areas such as biodiversity, clean air, water, waste management, and climate innovation. Rather than focusing on technical training, the programme emphasizes leadership development, collaboration, and systems thinking. What You Will Gain Participants will engage in a structured learning journey aimed at strengthening their leadership effectiveness. Key learning outcomes include: Developing self-awareness and clarity of purpose Building the ability to lead across sectors and diverse stakeholder groups Strengthening influence within complex systems Learning from experienced social sector leaders through curated sessions Engaging in peer learning, reflection, and real-time application The programme is highly interactive, combining workshops, discussions, and practical exercises to ensure deep learning. Programme Benefits ILACL offers a unique opportunity for climate professionals to grow both personally and professionally. Benefits include: Immersive in-person leadership training Access to experienced mentors and sector leaders Peer-to-peer learning with a diverse cohort Post-programme mentoring and alumni engagement opportunities Enhanced leadership capacity for real-world impact This experience is particularly valuable for those looking to step into greater leadership roles within the climate and sustainability space. Programme Details Dates: May 26 – May 29, 2026 Location: Bangalore, India Format: In-person (4-day intensive programme) The programme also includes follow-up mentoring and opportunities to reconnect with the alumni network. Who Should Apply This programme is ideal for high-potential individuals working in climate-related fields who are ready to take on greater leadership responsibilities. It is especially suited for professionals working in: Climate solutions and sustainability Environmental policy and advocacy Urban and rural development Social impact and nonprofit sectors How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official link . For more Leadership opportunities, visit OFY here. title: Smithsonian National Postal Museum Paid Summer Internships 2026: Strategy, Finance & Museum Operations Experience
summary: Applications are now open for paid summer internships at the Smithsonian National Postal Museum , part of the globally recognized Smithsonian Institution . These internships offer a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in museum operations, strategy, finance, and administration while working in Washington, D.C. , United States . About the Internship Program The Smithsonian National Postal Museum provides immersive internship experiences designed to give participants direct exposure to how a national museum operates behind the scenes. Interns will work alongside professionals across departments, contributing to real projects in planning, operations, and internal systems development. This program is ideal for individuals interested in museum studies, nonprofit management, business operations, and public sector careers. Internship Tracks Strategic Planning Internship The Strategic Planning Intern will work closely with the museum’s executive team and gain insight into high-level decision-making and operational planning. Key responsibilities include: Supporting strategic planning and management processes Conducting archival research and industry benchmarking Learning about museum leadership and operations Dates: June 22, 2026 – August 14, 2026 Eligibility: Open to undergraduate students and recent graduates (within 12 months of graduation) with an interest in: Museum studies Nonprofit management Business and fundraising Arts management History or government Finance Administration Internship This role focuses on operational and administrative support within the museum’s Finance and Administration department. Key responsibilities include: Supporting internal operations and process improvements Collaborating across departments Assisting with the development of an internal SharePoint system for document management Dates: June 8, 2026 – August 14, 2026 Eligibility: Open to undergraduate students and recent graduates (within 6 months of graduation) with an interest in: Museum studies Business administration or management Project management Human resources Internship Benefits Participants will gain valuable professional experience in a world-renowned institution. Benefits include: Paid internship opportunity Hands-on experience in museum operations and management Exposure to executive-level planning and organizational strategy Practical skills in research, administration, and collaboration Networking opportunities within the Smithsonian community Location All internships are in-person in Washington, D.C., providing a fully immersive experience within the museum environment. How to Apply Learn more. Apply via SOLAA (Program ID 2235). For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Click here. title: British Council TESOL Scholarships 2026 (Fully Funded): Study in the UK for Teachers in Africa, Europe and Asia
summary: The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a fully funded international scholarship programme designed to strengthen English language teaching (ELT) in government school systems across selected countries. Delivered in partnership with the University of Portsmouth and the University of Stirling , this programme offers 40 fully funded scholarships for experienced teachers and teacher educators to pursue a part-time, online Master’s degree in TESOL (Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages) while continuing to work in their home countries. Overview of the TESOL Scholarship Programme The scholarship supports educators from: Ghana Kenya Türkiye Bangladesh India Sri Lanka Participants will enroll in one of the following programmes: MA TESOL (Distance Learning) – University of Portsmouth (Ghana, Kenya, Türkiye) MSc TESOL (Online) – University of Stirling (Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka) The programme begins in September 2026 and runs part-time for up to 30 months , allowing scholars to balance work and study. Scholarship Benefits Each selected scholar will receive a comprehensive funding package, including: Full tuition fees for the TESOL master’s programme Return international flights to the UK Accommodation during the UK study visit Daily living stipend during the visit Visa application fee reimbursement Coverage of one English language test fee Additional Programme Benefits Access to world-class UK university faculty Professional mentoring and academic supervision International networking opportunities Membership in the British Council global alumni network UK Study Visit All scholars will participate in a fully funded two-week academic study visit to the United Kingdom , typically scheduled for Summer 2027 . This visit includes: In-person academic sessions Professional collaboration with peers Networking with global education experts Cultural exchange experiences Programme Structure The TESOL master’s programmes are delivered fully online , featuring: Weekly learning modules Asynchronous study materials Live virtual sessions (where applicable) One-to-one tutor support Dissertation supervision This flexible format allows participants to remain in their home countries while advancing their qualifications. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be citizens and permanent residents of an eligible country Work in or directly with the government education system Be active English language teachers or teacher educators Meet the academic requirements of the selected university Demonstrate commitment to improving national ELT systems Eligible Applicants Government school teachers Teacher trainers and educators Contract or temporary government teachers (if within the public system) Who Cannot Apply Private-sector teachers not affiliated with government systems Dual British citizens British Council or UK government employees (and close relatives) Individuals who studied in the UK within the past 5 years Applicants currently receiving UK government funding Individuals not residing in an eligible country Application Process Applicants must follow a two-step process : Step 1: Apply to a TESOL Master’s Programme Apply to the relevant university (Portsmouth or Stirling) Receive a conditional or unconditional offer Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship Submit the scholarship application after receiving the university offer Applications without a university offer will not be considered Applicants from Ghana, Kenya, and Türkiye: University of Portsmouth MA TESOL (Distance Learning) programme. Watch the information webinar. And apply or enquire. Applicants from Bangladesh, India, and Sri Lanka: University of Stirling MSc TESOL (Online) programme. Watch the information webinar. And apply or enquire. Required Documents For university admission, applicants typically need: Degree certificates and academic transcripts Curriculum Vitae (CV) Personal statement Academic or professional reference English language test score (if already available) English Language Requirements Not required at the initial application stage Required only after shortlisting Accepted tests include: IELTS TOEFL Pearson Cambridge English The scholarship covers one test attempt . Key Dates Application deadline: 30 April 2026 Programme start: September 2026 Scholarship decisions: End of June 2026 UK study visit: Summer 2027 Duration of Study University of Stirling: Up to 30 months University of Portsmouth: Up to 26 months Scholar Expectations After completing the programme, scholars are expected to: Continue working within the government education system Contribute to teacher development and training Strengthen English language teaching practices nationally Participate in British Council alumni activities Why Apply for the British Council TESOL Scholarships? This fully funded scholarship provides a unique opportunity to: Gain an internationally recognized TESOL master’s degree Enhance teaching practice and leadership skills Access global academic and professional networks Contribute to national education system improvement Learn more and apply on the official website here . Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Can I apply if I already have a Master’s degree? Yes, but priority is given to applicants without a TESOL or ELT master’s degree. Do I need to be in my home country? Yes. Applicants must live and work in their eligible country. Can I transfer previous ELT qualifications? No. Scholarship recipients must complete the full programme. Is the programme fully online? Yes, with one mandatory two-week UK study visit. Final Thoughts The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded opportunity for educators committed to transforming English language teaching systems. By combining flexible online learning, international exposure, and leadership development, this programme equips teachers with the skills and networks needed to drive meaningful educational change. If you want, I can also create: Learn more and apply on the official website here . Looking for more Scholarship opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website! title: Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany)
summary: The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026 . This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize , along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in mathematics and computer science. It provides a unique platform for journalists to engage directly with leading experts shaping the future of science. 🌟 About the Heidelberg Laureate Forum The Heidelberg Laureate Forum is a globally recognized networking conference that fosters dialogue between top-tier researchers and laureates in mathematics and computer science. Journalists selected for the program gain exclusive access to: In-depth discussions on cutting-edge research Direct interactions with globally recognized laureates Insights into emerging scientific trends A vibrant international network of experts and communicators 🎤 Special Highlight A key highlight of the 2026 forum is the Lindau Lecture , which will be delivered by Nobel Prize-winning physicist Brian Schmidt , renowned for discovering the accelerating expansion of the universe. 💰 Travel Grant Benefits Successful applicants will receive full financial support, including: Round-trip travel to Germany Accommodation throughout the forum Visa costs fully covered Full access to all sessions and networking opportunities This ensures participants can focus entirely on storytelling, learning, and professional growth. 📝 Application Process To apply for the travel grant: Create an account on the official application portal Enter your personal details (name, email, login credentials) Activate your account via the confirmation email Log in and complete your application form Submit before the deadline Ensure your application clearly demonstrates your experience in science or technology journalism and your motivation for attending. ⏰ Application Deadline The application period runs from March 2 to April 8, 2026 . Final Deadline: April 8, 2026 Applications submitted after this date will not be considered. 🚀 Why You Should Apply This opportunity offers immense value for journalists looking to elevate their careers: Access to exclusive, high-level scientific discussions Opportunities to produce impactful global stories Networking with top scientists and fellow journalists Exposure to groundbreaking developments in math and computer science 🔗 How to Apply Applicants should visit the official Heidelberg Laureate Forum website, create an account, and complete the application before the deadline. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more travel grant opportunities, click here . title: UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality
summary: Are you passionate about advancing gender equality and empowering women worldwide? The Government of Finland is offering an incredible opportunity for young professionals to join UN Women as a Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme Analyst in Lilongwe, Malawi. This prestigious role places you at the forefront of global development work, helping shape impactful programmes that uplift women economically and socially. 📌 About the Opportunity The UN Women JPO Programme Analyst (P2 level) position focuses on Women’s Economic Empowerment (WEE), a key priority area in Malawi. As part of this role, you will contribute to designing, implementing, and evaluating programmes that promote income security, decent work, and economic autonomy for women. Working closely with UN Women teams, government stakeholders, and international partners, you’ll help drive initiatives in areas such as: Entrepreneurship and skills development Digital inclusion and ICT for women Agricultural value chains and employment creation Policy development and gender equality frameworks This is a full-time, fixed-term position for 1 year , with the possibility of extension. 📍 Location Lilongwe, Malawi 🎯 Key Responsibilities As a Programme Analyst, you will: Support programme design, implementation, and evaluation Monitor results using Results-Based Management tools Provide technical assistance and capacity building to partners Coordinate programme activities and partnerships Contribute to reporting, budgeting, and donor engagement Support advocacy and knowledge-building initiatives 🎓 Eligibility Criteria To be considered, applicants must meet the following requirements: Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., economics, social sciences, gender studies, international development) At least 2 years of relevant professional experience Strong analytical, communication, and programme management skills Fluency in English (additional UN languages are an asset) 🌐 Why This Opportunity Matters Malawi presents a unique development context where women make up over half the population and contribute significantly to agriculture and the workforce—yet face systemic barriers in land ownership, finance, and leadership. By joining this programme, you will: Gain hands-on international development experience Work on impactful gender equality initiatives Build a global professional network Enhance your leadership and policy analysis skills 📅 Application Deadline April 16, 2026 (23:59 CET) 🔗 Application Link APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . 🚀 Final Thoughts This JPO position is more than just a job—it’s a gateway to a meaningful international career. If you are driven by purpose and ready to make a difference in women’s lives globally, this is your chance to step into a transformative role with UN Women. title: Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant  — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects
summary: Are you part of a research team working at the intersection of climate change and public health? The World Health Organization (WHO), through its Human Reproduction Programme (HRP), is inviting applications for a global call for research proposals focused on climate change and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRH) . This opportunity supports research teams in low- and middle-income countries to generate community-driven evidence on how climate change impacts SRH outcomes. With funding of up to USD 55,000 per project , this call is designed to strengthen research capacity while addressing urgent global health challenges through participatory and interdisciplinary approaches. About the Programme This call for proposals focuses on understanding how climate-related events — such as droughts, floods, and extreme temperatures — affect key areas of sexual and reproductive health and rights. These include maternal health, gender-based violence, access to contraception, and abortion care. Selected research teams will participate in a collaborative, cross-country research initiative. Rather than submitting a finalized protocol, applicants are asked to propose a research vision that demonstrates their approach, expertise, and connection to the communities they aim to study. A central component of the programme is a Protocol Development Workshop , scheduled to take place in Geneva in July 2026. During this workshop, selected teams will co-design a unified research framework and methodology. Travel and accommodation costs for up to two team members will be covered by WHO. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major global health challenge, yet its impact on sexual and reproductive health remains under-researched. This call aims to address critical evidence gaps while empowering local researchers and communities. By supporting participatory, community-led research, the programme ensures that those most affected by climate change play an active role in shaping solutions. This approach not only improves research quality but also enhances policy relevance and real-world impact. For research teams, this opportunity offers a pathway to contribute to global knowledge, influence policy, and strengthen long-term research capacity. What Participants Will Gain This opportunity provides both financial and institutional support to research teams working on critical global health issues. Selected teams will receive up to USD 55,000 in research funding , which can be used to support personnel, community engagement activities, data collection, and dissemination efforts. Beyond funding, participants will gain access to capacity-building resources , including training, technical guidance, and research tools provided by WHO. This support helps strengthen research design, implementation, and analysis capabilities. Teams will also benefit from international collaboration , working alongside other selected research groups across different countries. This cross-country exchange allows for shared learning, comparative analysis, and broader research impact. Another key benefit is access to WHO expertise and networks . Selected teams will receive guidance on ethics approval processes, data collection strategies, and dissemination, as well as connections to WHO country offices. Additionally, the programme supports participatory research approaches , meaning community members are actively involved as co-researchers. This enhances the relevance and impact of the research while strengthening community engagement. Who Can Apply This call is open to research teams based in low- and middle-income countries , particularly those in priority countries identified by WHO. Eligible applicants include: Academic and research institutions Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Community-based organizations and civil society groups Multi-institutional research consortia The Principal Investigator and core research team must be based in an eligible country. While international collaboration is encouraged, high-income country partners cannot lead the project and may receive no more than 15% of the total grant. Teams should demonstrate: Experience in SRH or climate-related research Expertise in participatory and mixed-methods approaches Strong community partnerships Capacity to conduct interdisciplinary research Research Focus Areas Applicants must select one of four priority research questions: Maternal health outcomes in the context of climate change Gender-based violence during climate-related events Access to contraception during climate shocks Access to abortion care in climate-affected settings All proposals must apply a human rights-based and intersectional approach , considering how factors such as gender, socioeconomic status, and migration influence vulnerability. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must submit their proposals through the official online platform, including all required documents such as budget, CVs, and conflict of interest declarations. Application Deadline: 12 April 2026 (23:59 GMT+1) Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. Selected teams will be notified by 11 May 2026 . Learn more and apply through the official WHO/HRP application platform . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities)
summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialized United Nations agency dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger. It does so by investing in rural people. IFAD finances programmes and projects that increase agricultural productivity and raise rural incomes, and advocates at the local, national and international level for policies that contribute to rural transformation. Job Role The regular Internship Programme (IP) and Enhanced Internship Programme (EIP) offer a unique learning experience of six months to recent university graduates or students who are currently specializing in areas of work relevant to IFAD s mission. Candidates for the Internship Programme must be 30 years old or less and must be enrolled in an accredited university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining the programme. Candidates must meet the following criteria: be enrolled in a university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining IFAD through the programme or; have recently completed university studies at the undergraduate or postgraduate level; be fluent in English; fluency in other IFAD official languages is an asset. In the case of interns recruited in IFAD country office, multi country office or regional office locations, fluency in the main country language may be required; be 30 years of age or younger. The Enhanced Internship Programme, in particular, is designed to enhance geographic distribution of staff at IFAD and is open to candidates who meet the above eligibility criteria and who are not based in Rome. Benefits include: Monthly stipend Housing and travel allowances (Enhanced Internship Programme) Mentorship, learning, and growth opportunities in a dynamic, global environment Financial support: In addition to a monthly allowance, Enhanced Internship Programme interns receive a housing and travel allowance. To apply, please login or register as an intern in IFAD s applicant system . Then click on ‘View all jobs’ and select the option under Job Opening Type, Internship Programme. For more info visit: https://www.ifad.org/en/internship-programme FAQS You may also be interested in this : Click here for Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome The post United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S.
summary: Applications are now open for the PAYA Youth Council , a paid leadership program under the Partnership to Advance Youth Apprenticeship (PAYA) . This unique opportunity is designed for current and former youth apprentices who are passionate about advocacy, leadership, and expanding access to apprenticeship opportunities across the United States. Participants will gain hands-on experience leading projects, influencing policy conversations, and working alongside peers from across the country—all while receiving a stipend. About the PAYA Youth Council The PAYA Youth Council is a leadership development initiative that empowers young people to take an active role in shaping the future of apprenticeship programs. Council members collaborate on youth-led projects, contribute to national conversations about workforce development, and advocate for improved access to career pathways. The program also creates a strong sense of community, bringing together young leaders committed to making a difference. What You Will Do As a member of the Youth Council, participants will: Develop advocacy and leadership skills Lead and contribute to youth-driven projects Engage in discussions on apprenticeship and workforce development Collaborate with peers from across the United States Represent youth voices in national conversations Participants will also have opportunities to share their ideas and create impact through real-world initiatives. Program Benefits The PAYA Youth Council offers both professional development and financial support. Benefits include: A generous stipend for participation Hands-on leadership and advocacy experience Networking opportunities with peers and professionals Participation in a summer celebration event in Washington, D.C. Exposure to national apprenticeship initiatives This is a valuable opportunity for young people looking to build leadership experience while contributing to meaningful change. Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about youth empowerment, workforce development, and creating opportunities for others, this program provides a platform to make your voice heard. Past participants have highlighted the impact of seeing their work contribute to real change and helping others access opportunities. Additional Opportunities Interested applicants can also: Attend an upcoming virtual info session to learn more about the program Explore past Youth Council projects Hear directly from former members about their experiences These resources provide insight into what to expect and how to make the most of the opportunity. How to Apply Apply here Info session Past projects Hear from past members For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.
